growth_records.cite: de Oliveira Lino et al. Strain dynamics of contaminating bacteria modulate the yield of ethanol biorefineries. Nature Communications (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41467-024-49683-2. [PMC11193817]
growth_records.notes: Growth rate at 30°C from OD-curve fitting (R package growthcurver) in SM medium, 96-deep-well plate, agitated; error = ±0.001 h-1. Value ordering (30°C first, then 37°C) inferred from the immediately preceding L. amylovorus sentence ('14% higher at 30°C, from 0.052±0.026 to 0.045±0.005 h-1', where 0.052/0.045=1.156, i.e., ~15.6% higher, consistent with the stated 14%). Note: text states 'all L. fermentum strains favoured the higher temperature' (i.e., higher mu at 37°C), which appears inconsistent with a decrease for strain A under this ordering — flagged as a possible ambiguity in the source text; values themselves are verbatim.

growth_records.cite: de Oliveira Lino et al. Strain dynamics of contaminating bacteria modulate the yield of ethanol biorefineries. Nature Communications (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41467-024-49683-2. [PMC11193817]
growth_records.notes: Growth rate at 37°C, same assay as the 30°C record for strain A. See ambiguity note in the 30°C record (this value is lower than the 30°C value, apparently contradicting the paper's stated overall trend of L. fermentum favouring higher temperature; reported verbatim regardless).

growth_records.cite: de Oliveira Lino et al. Strain dynamics of contaminating bacteria modulate the yield of ethanol biorefineries. Nature Communications (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41467-024-49683-2. [PMC11193817]
growth_records.notes: Growth rate at 30°C, OD-curve fitting (growthcurver), SM medium, 96-well plate assay.

growth_records.cite: de Oliveira Lino et al. Strain dynamics of contaminating bacteria modulate the yield of ethanol biorefineries. Nature Communications (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41467-024-49683-2. [PMC11193817]
growth_records.notes: Growth rate at 37°C, same assay as strain B 30°C record; consistent with paper's stated trend of L. fermentum favouring higher temperature.

growth_records.cite: de Oliveira Lino et al. Strain dynamics of contaminating bacteria modulate the yield of ethanol biorefineries. Nature Communications (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41467-024-49683-2. [PMC11193817]
growth_records.notes: Growth rate at 30°C, OD-curve fitting (growthcurver), SM medium, 96-well plate assay.

growth_records.cite: de Oliveira Lino et al. Strain dynamics of contaminating bacteria modulate the yield of ethanol biorefineries. Nature Communications (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41467-024-49683-2. [PMC11193817]
growth_records.notes: Growth rate at 37°C, same assay as strain C 30°C record; consistent with paper's stated trend of L. fermentum favouring higher temperature (~2.7-fold increase).

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 6460 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/6460
